description_long: This object consists of three tulip-like flowers attached to a single slender green stem, each flower displaying a unique coloration. The flowers are arranged vertically along the stem, with the topmost flower being orange with yellow accents, the middle flower a pale lavender, and the bottom flower a blend of orange and yellow. The petals are broad, slightly ruffled, and overlap in a whorled pattern, giving each bloom a full, layered appearance. Several elongated, lanceolate green leaves emerge from the stem, curving gently outward. The overall structure is delicate and upright, typical of ornamental flowers found in urban parks or landscaped gardens.
description: A vertical stem supports three colorful, ruffled tulip-like flowers with lanceolate green leaves. The blooms are orange, lavender, and yellow, arranged in a natural, layered fashion.
description_view_0: A side view showing the vertical arrangement of three flowers on a single stem, with visible overlapping leaves and the distinct coloration of each bloom.
description_view_1: A front view displaying all three flowers facing forward, clearly showing the differences in petal color and the symmetrical arrangement of the leaves.
description_view_2: A side view from the opposite direction, highlighting the profile of the flowers and the curvature of the leaves, with the color transitions between blooms visible.
description_view_3: A rear view showing the backs of the three flowers and the leaves, with the petal shapes and colors still discernible but less detailed than the front.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled configuration, with each flower displaying overlapping, slightly ruffled petals radiating symmetrically from the center. The arrangement is typical of tulip flowers, with a clear layering and radial symmetry.
